frase_1: Master's in Applied Computing in Artificial Intelligence
sobre1: Software Developer | Full Stack | React | Node | AI · Serra, Espírito Santo, Brazil.
sobre2: My journey in technology started in 2004 when I graduated as a computer technician from IFES. After 14 years in the administrative sector, I redirected my career in 2017 to software development.
sobre3: In 2020, I completed a Bachelor's in Information Systems at Unisales. Currently pursuing a Master's in IT at Ufes. Experienced in HTML, CSS, TypeScript, JavaScript, PHP, Python, React, React Native, Node.js and AI.
educacao1:
• Ufes — Master's in Information Technology (Sep/2025 - Mar/2026)
• IFES — Postgraduate, Cultural Maker & Education 4.0 (Feb/2025 - Jul/2025)
• IFES — Master's in Applied Computing, AI (Mar/2022 - Jul/2024)
• UniSales — Bachelor's in Information Systems (2017 - 2020)
• IFES — Computer Technician (2003 - 2004)
experiencia3e: Ecosoft Consultoria E Softwares Ambientais Ltda
project1d: Presence Now is a real-time facial recognition student attendance assessment software, FrontEnd was developed in react using typescript, BackEnd uses the same languages, and was implemented in the form of Rest API, the module face recognition uses only Javascript, HTML and CSS, but makes use of faceapi.js that uses the tensorflow.js API and implements a series of convulsional neural networks (CNNs).The system will basically register the student's presence through real-time facial recognition.
project2d:
Brazilian Food Guide application, developed for the TCC of Nutrition student Rafaela Soares Souza Kobi, the application was developed in React Native by FrameWork Expo and was published on Google PlayStore for Android devices. 
 Guia Application Alimentar Brasileiro, work that I was able to participate in, exposed at the XXVII Brazilian Congress of Nutrition, the largest congress in Latin America on Food and Nutrition, at the nutri saber fair.
project3d: Integrating project developed in the last period of graduation and together with Lucas Barbosa da Silva, using java by Android Studio.(Clone Show do Milhão).
project4d: An educational game related to traffic education with regard to traffic signs, was developed as a multimedia subject work, all of which were image and audio editing, languages ​​used Javascript, HTML and CSS.
project5d: Online Quiz Site developed for students of the Unisales Biomedicine Course.
project6d: Class notes and work developed in the master's degree in Artificial Intelligence at IFES - Instituto Federal do Espírito Santo.
project7d: Search Algorithms developed in Python for the Artificial Intelligence discipline of the master's degree at IFES .
project8d: FaceApi.js Test Site, using the tensorflow.js API and implementing a series of convulsive neural networks (CNNs).
project9t: Portfolio — Bruno Kobi Valadares de Amorim
project9d: Digital ecosystem built with React 18 + Vite 5 + TypeScript strict (zero errors). 9 languages with automatic IP detection. AI News Panel with 51 global RSS feeds, relevance scoring, anti-spam and auto-translation EN→PT. 3D map with ESRI ArcGIS. chatBruno Multi-Agent RAG (LangChain + pgvector + AWS EC2). 62 automated tests Vitest (71% coverage) + Playwright E2E. ESLint 9 + Prettier, zero errors. CI/CD GitHub Actions. Serverless deploy on Netlify.
project10d: Development of a gamified portfolio using React Three Fiber and Three.js. The project allows the user to control a UFO in a 3D environment to 'abduct' cows that represent my technological stacks.
project11d: Real-time 2D browser multiplayer game inspired by Tibia, with Brazilian political satire theme. Built with React, Phaser 4, Zustand and Supabase. In development.
project12d: Web-based sanitary audit system for verifying Good Manufacturing Practices (GMP). Features login, inspection management and compliance report generation.
project13d: Educational Machine Learning experiment analyzing the complete Mega-Sena lottery history (2,806 draws) using Gradient Boosting Classifier. Generates combinations with probability scores and statistical heatmaps.
project14d: Intelligent virtual assistant with Multi-Agent + RAG architecture, built on n8n, LangChain, Supabase pgvector and Google Gemini. 8 specialized agents (Profile, Skills, Experience, Education, Projects, Contact) routed by Gemini 2.5 Flash. Vector knowledge base of 22 chunks in Supabase. Self-hosted on AWS EC2.
project15t: Frota Realtime RJ — High-Performance GPS Fleet Tracking
project15d: +4,000 Rio de Janeiro buses tracked in real time via SPPO API. Serverless proxy compresses the payload by 70%. requestAnimationFrame interpolation ensures smooth 60 FPS animation. Speed-based color coding processed on the GPU via MapLibre GL. Auto-clustering by zoom level and 10-position trail per bus. localStorage fallback with age indicator on API failure.
city_san_francisco_desc: The world's leading innovation hub, home to the largest venture capital ecosystem and pioneering companies in artificial intelligence. It is the birthplace of Google, Apple, Meta and Airbnb, which continue to shape the global technology landscape from this iconic city.
city_nova_york_desc: The global financial capital, where Wall Street's power meets a thriving technology ecosystem known as Silicon Alley. The city leads in fintech innovation and rapid AI deployment across financial services, media and enterprise software.
city_londres_desc: Europe's premier technology ecosystem, globally recognized for pioneering AI regulation, fintech innovation and decentralized finance. London attracts billions in venture capital annually and hosts the continent's largest concentration of tech talent.
city_toquio_desc: Asia's pioneering technology capital, leading the world in robotics, automation, smart mobility and urban internet infrastructure. Tokyo combines deep industrial expertise with cutting-edge consumer tech, setting global standards in precision engineering and electronics.
city_seul_desc: Known as the city of the future, Seoul dominates in 5G connectivity, hardware manufacturing, IoT and digital public services. It is home to global tech giants Samsung and LG, driving innovation across consumer electronics and semiconductors.
city_singapura_desc: Asia's leading smart city excels in artificial intelligence investment, digital government and attracting top international talent. Its strategic location and business-friendly policies make it the preferred regional hub for tech companies expanding across Southeast Asia.
city_shenzhen_desc: The world's Silicon Valley of Hardware, Shenzhen is the global epicenter of electronics manufacturing, rapid prototyping and industrial innovation. Home to Huawei, Tencent and DJI, the city transforms tech concepts into mass-market products faster than anywhere else on earth.
city_bangalore_desc: Known as India's Silicon Valley, Bangalore hosts the world's largest concentration of IT outsourcing and software services talent. The city is home to India's fastest-growing startup ecosystem, with over 12,000 active startups spanning AI, SaaS, fintech and deeptech.
city_berlim_desc: One of Europe's largest startup hubs, Berlin thrives on a culture of open technology, creativity and entrepreneurial spirit. The city is a magnet for global tech talent and home to major companies including Zalando, SoundCloud and Delivery Hero.
city_sao_paulo_desc: Latin America's largest tech and innovation engine, São Paulo hosts the highest concentration of fintechs and unicorns in the region. The city drives digital transformation across Brazil and neighboring countries with a vibrant startup ecosystem backed by world-class venture capital.
city_tel_aviv_desc: Known as Silicon Wadi, Tel Aviv is a global leader in cybersecurity, defense technology and deep artificial intelligence. Despite its small size, Israel produces more startups per capita than almost any other country, driven by elite military tech training programs.
city_paris_desc: A major European tech hub in rapid ascent, Paris excels in advanced AI research and massive government investment in the tech sector. The city is home to Station F, the world's largest startup campus, and hosts research centers for DeepMind, Meta AI and other global AI leaders.
city_amsterda_desc: A strategic European hub for semiconductor companies, smart logistics, cloud computing and green technology. Amsterdam's exceptional internet infrastructure and business-friendly environment attract major tech companies as their European headquarters.
city_toronto_desc: North America's third-largest tech hub, Toronto is globally recognized for pioneering academic research in neural networks and artificial intelligence. The city's universities and research institutes have produced foundational AI breakthroughs that power systems around the world.
city_sydney_desc: Oceania's primary tech engine, Sydney concentrates the region's most successful unicorns in enterprise software, digital design and fintech. The city attracts global tech companies as their Asia-Pacific headquarters, benefiting from Australia's stable economy and skilled workforce.
city_xangai_desc: An Asian giant focused on semiconductors, autonomous electric vehicles, high-scale e-commerce and supercomputing. Shanghai drives China's industrial technology ambitions, combining state-of-the-art manufacturing with world-class R&D centers.
city_austin_desc: Known as Silicon Hills, Austin attracts hardware and software giants due to its competitive cost of living and vibrant innovation culture. The city became Tesla's headquarters and home to major expansions by Apple, Google and Oracle, cementing its role as America's emerging tech capital.
city_estocolmo_desc: Europe's unicorn factory, Stockholm is famous for producing digital giants in streaming, gaming and digital payments despite its relatively small population. Home to Spotify, Klarna, King and Mojang, it creates more billion-dollar startups per capita than any European city except London.
city_pequim_desc: China's academic and research powerhouse, Beijing hosts the headquarters of the country's largest internet companies including Baidu, ByteDance and Xiaomi. The city receives massive government investment in AI, quantum computing and digital infrastructure, positioning it as a global tech superpower.
city_munique_desc: A hub of advanced industrial technology, Munich leads in digital automotive engineering, industrial IoT and aerospace innovation. Home to BMW, Siemens and MAN, the city combines traditional German engineering excellence with cutting-edge digital transformation.
city_lagos_desc: Considered Africa's startup capital, Lagos hosts the continent's largest fintech ecosystem, with companies like Paystack and Flutterwave achieving global recognition. The city is the epicenter of digital revolution in Sub-Saharan Africa, attracting billions in venture capital and producing a new generation of tech entrepreneurs.
city_nairobi_desc: Nicknamed Silicon Savannah, Nairobi is the birthplace of M-Pesa, the mobile payment system that revolutionized financial access across Africa. The city leads the continent in mobile banking adoption, with a rapidly growing startup ecosystem focused on AI, agritech and mobile money.
city_cairo_desc: With the Arab world's largest population and a growing, highly connected young workforce, Cairo is emerging as North Africa's leading tech hub. The city hosts hundreds of e-commerce, edtech and healthtech startups, driven by strong government investment and international accelerators.
city_cidade_do_cabo_desc: Southern Africa's premier innovation hub, Cape Town leads in startups addressing renewable energy, fintech and software for emerging markets. The local ecosystem blends creative talent, world-class infrastructure and strong connections with European and American investors.
city_dubai_desc: Dubai has established itself as the global laboratory for smart cities, deploying cutting-edge technologies in autonomous transportation, government blockchain and fully digital public services. Through the Dubai Future Foundation, it attracts startups and multinationals worldwide seeking to build the urban future.
city_riad_desc: Driven by the ambitious Vision 2030 plan, Riyadh is investing tens of billions in economic diversification through technology, AI and the digital economy. The city attracts global tech giants and local startups, rapidly becoming one of the Middle East's largest emerging technology markets.
city_jacarta_desc: As the capital of the world's largest archipelago, Jakarta hosts Southeast Asia's most dynamic startup ecosystem, having produced unicorns like Gojek, Tokopedia and Bukalapak. The city leads Indonesia's digital transformation, leveraging 270 million increasingly connected citizens eager for digital solutions.
city_kuala_lumpur_desc: Strategically positioned at the heart of Southeast Asia, Kuala Lumpur is a hub for digital financial services, cybersecurity and regional software development. The city attracts multinational tech companies as their regional base and invests heavily in digital talent through the MSC Malaysia corridor.
city_ho_chi_minh_desc: Known as Vietnam's economic engine, Ho Chi Minh City is rapidly growing as a nearshoring destination for software development, serving American and European companies with high quality at competitive costs. The city also nurtures a vibrant local startup scene in logtech, fintech and edtech.
city_taipei_desc: Taipei is the strategic heart of the global semiconductor supply chain, home to TSMC, the world's largest chip manufacturer, producing processors for Apple, NVIDIA and AMD. The city represents the most critical link in the global hardware supply chain, combining cutting-edge research with unprecedented industrial capacity.
city_tallinn_desc: Tallinn is globally recognized as the most advanced model of digital government, offering nearly 100% of public services online and pioneering the concept of digital e-Residency. It is also the birthplace of Skype, Wise and Pipedrive, making it a disproportionately large startup hub for its population size.
city_varsovia_desc: Warsaw is emerging as the largest tech hub in Central and Eastern Europe, housing R&D centers for multinationals such as Google, Microsoft and Samsung. The city combines a strong academic tradition in computer science with competitive living costs and a rapidly growing tech workforce.
city_zurique_desc: Zurich is simultaneously Europe's leading financial center and the heart of the Swiss Crypto Valley, home to some of the world's largest blockchain and Web3 organizations. The city also hosts ETH Zurich, one of the world's most renowned universities in computing and AI.
city_seattle_desc: Seattle is home to two of the world's largest technology companies, Amazon and Microsoft, and one of the densest software engineering ecosystems on the planet. The city is a global reference in cloud computing, having been the birthplace of both AWS and Azure.
city_boston_desc: Boston is the world's largest hub for biotech and life sciences, concentrating hundreds of pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies around MIT and Harvard. The city also leads in AI, robotics and quantum computing research, with an academic talent density unmatched by any other American city.
city_miami_desc: Miami has transformed into the United States' leading hub for crypto and Web3, attracting venture capital funds, exchanges and blockchain projects from around the world. The city also emerges as a gateway for Latin American startups seeking US market access, combining world-class infrastructure with unique cultural ties to Latin America.
city_cidade_do_mexico_desc: Mexico City is Latin America's second largest startup ecosystem, with explosive growth in fintech, e-commerce and logtech. Its privileged position as a bridge between the American and Latin American markets attracts global investors and multinationals seeking to penetrate the Hispanic market.
city_vancouver_desc: Vancouver is the world capital of video game production and visual effects, hosting major studios from EA, Ubisoft and Microsoft. The city is also consolidating as an AI hub, attracting corporate research centers drawn by the exceptional talent from the University of British Columbia.
city_buenos_aires_desc: Buenos Aires has one of the most skilled developer communities in Latin America, globally recognized for expertise in software, digital design and artificial intelligence. The city is also one of the region's largest crypto and blockchain centers, with very high cryptocurrency adoption driven by historical economic instability.
city_bogota_desc: Bogota is consolidating as Latin America's third largest startup hub, with accelerating growth in fintech, logtech and digital health solutions. The city benefits from a mature ecosystem of accelerators, venture capital funds and innovation-friendly government policies.
city_melbourne_desc: Melbourne is Australia's second largest technology hub, with a strong presence in fintech, gaming and enterprise software development. The city hosts a growing ecosystem of AI and digital health startups, driven by renowned research universities and immigration policies that attract global tech talent.
